title: linaria supina var. hegelmaieri
intro: Linaria supina var. hegelmaieri, also known as the dwarf toadflax, is a flowering plant in the figwort family that is native to Europe and western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Linaria supina var. hegelmaieri include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its use in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea or tincture, and can also be made into a salve or poultice for topical application.
